candidates.note: _UI Sandbox uses neutrals only; nextjs-saas uses semantic 'primary' token

primary.CONFLICT: appy-next (teal-500) vs docs-next (blue-600) vs _UI Sandbox (neutral-only) vs nextjs-saas (semantic 'primary')

neutral.CONFLICT: Three different neutral palettes: gray (appy-next), slate/zinc (_UI Sandbox), slate (docs-next), semantic (nextjs-saas)

error.note: Not explicitly used in high-frequency utility analysis

surface.consensus: ALL KITS AGREE - white is surface color (frequency 154-179)

spacing.consensus: 100% AGREEMENT - All kits use identical Tailwind spacing scale

typography.consensus: 100% AGREEMENT - All kits use Tailwind defaults, no custom typography detected

details._UI Sandbox: no primary - neutral-first design using slate/zinc/neutral
details.nextjs-saas-starter: semantic token 'primary' (freq: 51) - no concrete color mapped

conflicts.impact: Impossible to build consistent primary component without choosing one approach

details._UI Sandbox: slate-300 through slate-900 + zinc-100 through zinc-900 + neutral-600/950
details.nextjs-saas-starter: semantic names (muted, foreground, border) with no concrete colors in utility analysis

conflicts.impact: Neutral colors foundational to contrast ratios and accessibility - must align

details.group_1: appy-next, docs-next, _UI Sandbox use concrete Tailwind color names (teal-500, gray-800, slate-700, etc)
details.group_2: nextjs-saas-starter uses semantic naming (primary, accent, muted, background, foreground, border, destructive, sidebar, input, popover)

conflicts.impact: CANNOT MIX APPROACHES - creates two incompatible token systems. Must choose one globally.
conflicts.recommendation: Adopt semantic tokens (nextjs-saas-starter pattern). More maintainable, brand-agnostic, easier to theme later.

conflicts.impact: Secondary UI elements appear inconsistent across kits

summary.recommendation: Adopt semantic token architecture. Map: primary=teal-500 or blue-600 (choose), neutral=slate (most common), accent=purple-500, error=pink-500
